name: Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce
name_original: Cascada de Augacaída y Castro de Marce – circular desde Marce
region_intro: Hiking around Pantón, Galicia, offers diverse trails through a landscape shaped by the Miño and Sil rivers. The region is characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and deep river canyons, including the ZEC Canón do Sil. Ancient settlements and waterfalls are common features along many routes, providing points of interest for hikers. The terrain varies from gentle paths suitable for easy hikes around Pantón to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Pantón The most popular hiking route is Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce, a 3.1 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This easy route explores a notable waterfall and an ancient fort. Another top favourite among local hikers is Augacaída Waterfall loop from Torre de Marce, a moderate 2.2 miles (3.6 km) path. This trail offers views of the Augacaída Waterfall within a forested setting. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from ZEC Canón do Sil, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through the protected ZEC Canón do Sil area, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Pantón is defined by river canyons, waterfalls, and historical sites, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Pantón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Pantón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Pantón?
A: Pantón offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging routes, exploring the diverse landscape of the Miño and Sil river valleys.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Pantón?
A: Yes, Pantón has 23 easy hiking trails. An excellent option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979353635/augacaida-waterfall-and-castro-de-marce-circular-from-marce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.1 miles (4.9 km) long and explores a waterfall and an ancient fort.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Pantón?
A: Hike durations in Pantón vary significantly. Many popular routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38256817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall loop from Torre de Marce&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in under 2 hours. Longer, more challenging trails may take 3-4 hours or more, depending on your pace and the terrain.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Pantón?
A: Many of the easier trails in Pantón are suitable for families. Routes that feature points of interest like the Augacaída Waterfall or ancient settlements are particularly engaging for children. Always check the specific trail's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Pantón?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Pantón, especially in rural and natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, particularly near livestock or in protected natural zones like the ZEC Canón do Sil, to respect wildlife and other hikers.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike in Pantón?
A: The best time to hike in Pantón is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the natural scenery, especially the changing leaves in autumn, is particularly beautiful. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild but can be wet.

Q: Are there circular walks available in Pantón?
A: Yes, Pantón offers several excellent circular walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24309657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from ZEC Canón do Sil&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 4.2-mile (6.8 km) route that allows you to experience the protected river canyon without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or viewpoints can I expect to see along the trails?
A: Pantón's trails often lead to stunning natural attractions and viewpoints. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5016863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or explore various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the Miño and Sil river canyons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cividade Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Longa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, Pantón is rich in history. Many trails pass by ancient settlements like Castro de Marce, and you can also find historical religious buildings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, a unique rock-hewn monastery, is a notable historical landmark in the wider Ribeira Sacra area.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Pantón?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points for hikes, especially those leading to well-known attractions like the Augacaída Waterfall or within the ZEC Canón do Sil area, often have designated parking spaces. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pantón?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Pantón, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, including the dramatic river canyons and tranquil woodlands, as well as the presence of historical sites and beautiful waterfalls.

Q: Are there any hidden gem trails or less-known spots in Pantón?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by individual exploration, Pantón's less-trafficked trails, particularly those deeper within the ZEC Canón do Sil or along smaller tributaries, can offer a sense of solitude and unexpected natural beauty away from the more popular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall – Castro de Marce Viewpoint loop from Torre de Marce&lt;/a&gt; offers a slightly different perspective of a well-known area.
tips:
The castro de Marce, in the municipality of Pantón, offers beautiful views of the area, the Miño river and the Os Peares reservoir.
This prominent point on the Miño was the home of the xacios or aquatic men, according to local traditions. Thanks to its elevated position, it was possible to monitor a large area of the banks and the old port of Chouzán, on the opposite bank of the river, thus being a very important strategic point.

The Augacaída fervenza is located in the Ribeira Sacra of Lugo, in the parish of Marce in the municipality of Pantón. This fervenza, a word that in Galician designates a waterfall, is largely unknown, even to people from Galicia, but the truth is that it is one of the highest in the community, since its waters fall from more than 40 meters Tall.
